data.title: EOG Resources reports 2Q 2025 results with $5.48B revenue, $1.35B net income ($2.46 per share), and $1B free cash flow. 2025 guidance updated post Encino acquisition. To learn more visit www.eogresources.com.
data.description: EOG Resources, Inc. reported second quarter 2025 results with total revenue at $5.478 billion, net income of $1.345 billion, and net cash provided by operating activities at $2.032 billion. The company had total expenditures of $1.883 billion, with current and long-term debt at $4.236 billion. EOG generated $973 million in free cash flow, paid $528 million in dividends, and repurchased $600 million of shares. They completed a $3.5 billion debt offering for the Encino acquisition. For 2025, EOG updated its guidance and expects total capital expenditures of $6.2-$6.4 billion. The company also repurchased 5.4 million shares for $600 million during the second quarter.

data.title: NWPX Infrastructure, Inc. reports record sales, $133.2M; net income $9.1M; backlog $348M. WTS net sales $84.6M; Precast $48.6M.
data.description: NWPX Infrastructure, Inc. announced its financial results for Q2 2025, with net sales of $133.2 million, a 2.8% increase YoY. The Precast Infrastructure segment reported record net sales of $48.6 million. Net income was $0.91 per diluted share. The Water Transmission Systems segment had a backlog of $298 million. The Precast segment had an order book of $56 million. The Company completed corporate rebranding to NWPX Infrastructure, Inc. The WTS segment had a 5.5% decrease in net sales, while Precast segment's net sales increased by 21.5%. The Company had $30.6 million in outstanding revolving loan borrowings. Net cash provided by operating activities was $5.4 million for Q2 2025. The Company also repurchased $15.0 million of common stock. The Company will hold a conference call on August 8, 2025.

data.title: American Healthcare REIT reports Q2 2025 results, including $9.9M net income. Increases 2025 guidance with total investments of $255M. Stock issuance raises $188.6M. Net-debt-to-EBITDA improves from 4.5x to 3.7x. Strong growth in Same-Store NOI leads to raised expectations. Acquisition details also provided, showcasing strong financial performance and strategic growth. CEO expresses confidence in company's healthcare mission.
data.description: American Healthcare REIT, Inc. reported Q2 2025 results with a GAAP net income of $9.9 million. They achieved a 13.9% portfolio Same-Store Net Operating Income growth in Q2 2025. During the same period, they acquired a $65 million SHOP asset, totaling $255 million of new investments in 2025. The Company issued over 5.4 million common stock shares for $188.6 million in Q2 2025 and increased their full-year 2025 guidance for NOI growth and NFFO. They reported a Net Debt-to-Annualized Adjusted EBITDA improvement from 4.5x to 3.7x. The Company received a Great Place To Work Certification. CEO expressed confidence in the long-term care industry.

data.title: VIAVI reported fiscal year 2025 revenue of $1.08B, up 8.4%, with Q4 net revenue of $290.5M, up 15.3%. GAAP net income increased 136.9% YoY to $8.0M in Q4. Operating margins rose, CEO optimistic for continued growth in 2025. Company holds $429.0M in cash, generated $89.8M in cash flows in FY 2025. expects Q1 2026 revenue between $290M-$298M. Non-GAAP EPS to be $0.13-$0.14. For additional details, visit VIAVI's press release.
data.description: VIAVI reported its fiscal fourth quarter and fiscal year results for 2025. In the fourth quarter, net revenue was $290.5 million, up 15.3% year-over-year, with GAAP operating margin at 5.3%. Non-GAAP operating margin was 14.4%, GAAP net income at $8.0 million, and Non-GAAP net income at $29.7 million, up 73.7% year-over-year. For the full fiscal year 2025, net revenue was $1.08 billion, a year-over-year increase of 8.4%. GAAP operating margin was 5.3%, and Non-GAAP operating margin was 14.2%. GAAP net income was $34.8 million, up 234.9% year-over-year, and Non-GAAP net income was $105.4 million, up 43.4% year-over-year. The company's President and CEO, Oleg Khaykin, noted positive momentum in VIAVI's end markets and expected it to continue. The company generated $23.8 million and $89.8 million in cash flows from operations during the fiscal quarter and year, respectively. For the first quarter of fiscal 2026, net revenue is expected to be between $290 million to $298 million. VIAVI Solutions is a global provider of network test, monitoring, and assurance solutions. Further details of the financial tables and the forward-looking statements are available in the original press release.
Ouster reports Q2 2025 results: $35M revenue, up 30% YoY, 7% sequentially; GAAP gross margin 45%.

data.title: Nektar Therapeutics reported financial results: Q2 2025 cash $175.9M, down from $269.1M in Dec. 2024. Revenue Q2 2025 $11.2M vs. $23.5M in Q2 2024. Operating costs Q2 2025 $47.4M vs. $73.3M in Q2 2024. Net loss Q2 2025 $41.6M vs. $52.4M in Q2 2024. Key highlights include rezpegaldesleukin progress and new programs development. FDA granted Fast Track designation for alopecia areata treatment. Public stock offering raised $115M. Conference call to review results on August 7, 2025.
data.description: Nektar Therapeutics reported financial results for Q2 2025 with $175.9 million in cash and investments. They expect these funds, along with a recent secondary offering of $107.5 million, to support operations into Q1 2027. Revenue decreased to $11.2 million in Q2 2025 from $23.5 million in Q2 2024 due to the sale of a manufacturing facility, while operating costs decreased to $47.4 million in Q2 2025 from $73.3 million in Q2 2024. Research and development expenses slightly increased to $29.9 million in Q2 2025. Net loss was $41.6 million in Q2 2025. Nektar's lead product candidate, rezpegaldesleukin, showed positive results in atopic dermatitis and alopecia areata studies. FDA granted Fast Track designation for rezpegaldesleukin. They also announced the successful public offering of common stock raising $115 million. Nektar plans to advance new programs like NKTR-0165 and NKTR-0166. Total assets on June 30, 2025, were $186.6 million. Nektar remains focused on developing treatments for autoimmune and chronic inflammatory diseases.

data.title: Erie Indemnity's Q2 net income was $174.7M with $3.34 per diluted share, improving from the same period in 2024; 1H net income was $313.1M.
data.description: Erie Indemnity Company reported a net income of $174.7 million ($3.34 per diluted share) for the second quarter of 2025, compared to $163.9 million ($3.13 per diluted share) in 2024. For the first six months of 2025, the net income was $313.1 million ($5.99 per diluted share), up from $288.5 million ($5.52 per diluted share) in 2024. Operating income before taxes increased by $9.0 million in Q2 2025 compared to Q2 2024. Management fee revenues saw a growth of $63.0 million in the second quarter and $152.3 million in the first six months of 2025. Commissions increased by $43.5 million in Q2 and $104.6 million for the first half of 2025. Income from investments totaled $19.6 million in Q2 and $39.1 million for the first half of 2025. Erie Insurance Group, founded in 1925, is a Fortune 500 company with more than 7 million policies in force and operates in 12 states and D.C.

data.title: Get the scoop on GPT-5: OpenAI's latest language model is out with 13 key points to know.
data.description: The unveiling of GPT-5, OpenAI's latest cutting-edge language model, took place on Thursday. This new version represents a significant advancement in artificial intelligence technology. GPT-5 is equipped with state-of-the-art capabilities that set it apart from its predecessors. Users are encouraged to explore the features and functions of this innovative model to fully grasp its potential.
Trump On India Tariff: No Talks Until Issues Are Resolved

data.title: Trump nominating Stephen Miran, CEA chair, for Fed Board, serving until 2026. Known for pro-tariff stance. From investment background to policy maker.
data.description: President Donald Trump announced his nomination of Stephen Miran, the current chair of the Council of Economic Advisers, to fill the vacant Federal Reserve Board seat previously held by Adriana Kugler. If approved by the Senate, Miran is set to serve until January 31, 2026. This appointment provides Miran with a substantial but time-limited opportunity to participate in shaping U.S. monetary policy. Miran's background saw him as a relatively unknown figure in U.S. policymaking until recently, having worked in investment firms. His advocacy for pro-tariff policies, notably outlined in a 41-page trade strategy document named the "Mar-a-Lago Accord," drew Trump's attention. Miran's ascent culminated in December when Trump designated him to lead the CEA. For more details, the full article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: JBT Marel Corporation declares $0.10 quarterly dividend per share, payable on Sept 2, 2025, to stockholders of record on Aug 18.
data.description: In Chicago, JBT Marel Corporation (NYSE and Nasdaq Iceland: JBTM) disclosed a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share for its common stock. This dividend is scheduled for payment on September 2, 2025, to shareholders listed on record as of August 18, 2025. JBT Marel Corporation (NYSE and Nasdaq Iceland: JBTM) is recognized as a top technology solutions provider for premium sectors within the food & beverage industry.

data.title: SentinelOne (NYSE: S) to release Q2 2026 financial results on Aug 28, 2025, with conference call at 1:30 p.m. PT. Also presenting at investor conference.
data.description: Mountain View, California – SentinelOne, Inc. disclosed that it is set to unveil its financial outcomes for the fiscal Q2 of 2026, concluding on July 31, 2025. The results are expected to be made public post the market's closure on Thursday, August 28, 2025. A conference call has been scheduled by SentinelOne to delve into the results at 1:30 p.m. PT (4:30 p.m. ET) on the same date. Moreover, the company is slated to participate in the investor conference known as Goldman Sachs Communacopia + Technology Co.

data.title: Eventbrite (NYSE: EB) reports Q2 2025 results on June 30, seen as positive performance with sustainable improvement.
data.description: In San Francisco, Eventbrite (NYSE: EB), a worldwide platform for shared experiences, released its financial data for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. The Company's Q2 Investor Presentation is available on their Investor Relations site at https://investor.eventbrite.com. According to Julia Har, the results highlight effective execution leading to operational success and consistent growth in profitability for the company.

data.title: Grindr Inc. announces 27% revenue growth in Q2 2025, on track to achieve 2025 outlook. CEO George Arison positive.
data.description: In Los Angeles, California, Grindr Inc. (NYSE: GRND) announced its financial performance for the quarter ending on June 30, 2025. The company, known as the Global Gayborhood in Your Pocket™, shared its results in a Letter to Shareholders accessible on its Investor Relations website. CEO George Arison expressed satisfaction with the quarter, stating that Grindr remains on course to meet its targets for 2025. The detailed financial information can be found on the company's investor website at https://investors.grindr.com/.

data.title: Atlassian reports $5.2 billion revenue for FY25. Leader in team collaboration and productivity software.
data.description: In a recent announcement from Team Anywhere based in San Francisco, Atlassian Corporation (NASDAQ: TEAM) revealed their financial performance for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ending June 30, 2025. The company reported over $5.2 billion in revenue for FY25. A detailed shareholder letter discussing these results can be accessed on the Investor Relations page of Atlassian’s website at https://investors.atlassian.com. This marks a significant achievement for the provider of team collaboration and productivity software.

data.title: Owlet reports Q2 2025 financial results, increases full year guidance, and CEO transition plan. Conference call on Aug 7 at 4:30 pm ET.
data.description: In Lehi, Utah, Owlet, Inc. (NYSE: OWLT), a leading smart infant monitoring company, recently shared its financial performance for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. CEO and Co-Founder Kurt Workman, President Jonathan Harris, and CFO Amanda Twede Crawford will lead a conference call on August 7, 2025, at 4:30 p.m. ET to discuss the results and offer business insights. The call will cover the Q2 2025 financials, indicating significant company updates and growth in this period.

data.title: Chime (Nasdaq: CHYM) reported strong Q2 2025 results with accelerating growth & expanding margins. CEO Chris Britt upbeat.
data.description: In San Francisco, Chime® (Nasdaq: CHYM), a prominent consumer financial technology company, released its financial outcomes for the quarter concluding on June 30, 2025. CEO and Co-founder Chris Britt expressed, "This initial quarter as a publicly traded company marked a significant growth period for Chime, with a surge in year-over-year growth, improved margins, and ongoing product advancements." Britt emphasized the robustness of Chime's payments-centric approach, driven by consistent revenue streams and extensive product development efforts.

data.title: Blend Labs announces Jason Ream joining as Head of Finance by August 8, 2025, with over 25 years of financial expertise.
data.description: In San Francisco, Blend Labs, Inc. (NYSE: BLND), a prominent digital origination platform for financial institutions, disclosed the appointment of Jason Ream as their Head of Finance and Administration starting August 8, 2025. With more than 25 years of experience in financial and operational roles within the technology and software sectors, Jason Ream previously held the position of CFO at two publicly traded firms including SailPoint Technologies. This move signifies a strategic step for Blend Labs to enhance its financial operations and leadership team.

data.title: Clean Energy reported $102.6M in revenue and sold 61.4M RNG gallons in Q2 2025, with a net loss of $(20.2M).
data.description: Clean Energy Fuels Corp. released its second-quarter 2025 operating results. The company reported a revenue of $102.6 million in Q2 2025, up from $98.0 million in Q2 2024. However, they also announced a net loss attributable to Clean Energy of $(20.2) million, or $(0.09) per share, for Q2 2025 on a GAAP basis. This is compared to $(16.3) million, or $(0.07) per share, in Q2 2024. Additionally, Clean Energy reported their Adjusted EBITDA for the period but did not provide specific figures.
